description: A SMALL, illustrative snapshot of real legal authorities used to demonstrate the legal_citation_exists verifier (the Mata v. Avianca killer) across federated sources. In production this list must be populated from authoritative primary sources via the live resolver: Hong Kong e-Legislation (elegislation.gov.hk) + HKLII (hklii.hk) for HK; the UK National Archives 'Find Case Law' (caselaw.nationalarchives.gov.uk) for UK; and CourtListener (courtlistener.com) for US. This file is a stand-in, NOT a citator. Every entry is a real, publicly reported authority; do not treat the snapshot as exhaustive.

provenance.hkCaseLaw: HKLII (hklii.hk), Free Access to Law Movement
provenance.hkLegislation: Hong Kong e-Legislation (elegislation.gov.hk), Department of Justice
provenance.note: Verified-against-an-official-reporter is the user's non-delegable duty — see docs/08-Domains/Legal-Industry-Fit.md.

authorities.name: Yu Hon Tong Thomas v Centaline Property Agency Ltd
authorities.note: Court criticised AI-drafted submissions for making up allegations without evidential basis.
authorities.holding: The court criticised submissions that appeared to be artificially constructed using AI and that made up severe allegations without any evidential basis; it did not endorse those allegations.

authorities.name: Licksun Company Ltd v Occupiers of Lot No. 552 in D.D. 85
authorities.note: Court expressed serious concern over non-existent authorities cited by a litigant in person.

authorities.name: Ayinde v Haringey; Al-Haroun v Qatar National Bank
authorities.note: Heard under the Hamid jurisdiction; fabricated and misstated authorities.

authorities.name: R (Miller) v The Prime Minister; Cherry v Advocate General for Scotland
authorities.holding: The advice to prorogue Parliament was unlawful because it had the effect of frustrating or preventing Parliament's ability to carry out its constitutional functions without reasonable justification; the prorogation was null and of no effect.

authorities.name: Mata v. Avianca, Inc.
authorities.holding: The court imposed Rule 11 sanctions on attorneys who submitted a brief citing non-existent, AI-fabricated cases and then stood by them when challenged; it concerns sanctions for fake citations, not aviation liability.

authorities.name: Obergefell v. Hodges
authorities.court: Supreme Court of the United States
authorities.note: Real SCOTUS authority (2015) — a positive control for the US source.
authorities.holding: The Fourteenth Amendment requires States to license and recognise marriages between two people of the same sex; same-sex couples have a fundamental right to marry.

authorities.name: Legislation Publication Ordinance
authorities.note: Gives verified e-Legislation copies legal status.

authorities.name: Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance (PDPO)
authorities.note: Technology-neutral; six Data Protection Principles. Section 33 not yet in force.
